page_content:
### **3. Trevor Exley (Lab Supervisor)**
*(Friendly, authentic, warm — like a real student writing to an old mentor)*  
**Subject:** Quick Update — Arian Moridani  
Hi Dr. Exley (Trevor),  
Hope your postdoc in Italy is going great and that you’re enjoying the new place! I’ve seen you’ve been doing some amazing work there through linkedin and ARXIV.  
I wanted to share a quick update: I’m applying to medical school this cycle, and I’m finishing up my **M.S. in Computer Science (Machine Learning)** at Georgia Tech this summer. My goal is to bring data-driven tools like AI, big data, and computer vision into robotics and clinical adjacent settings — something that really resonated with me after seeing how you studied AI before your PhD.  
Seeing how your path shaped your research inspired me to take a similar route: master the technical side of machine learning first, so I can become a physician who doesn’t just treat patients — but helps them through smarter diagnostics and data systems.  
I’m reaching out to Dr. Jafari for a letter now, and since we worked on the **TVIM publication** (especially the MATLAB filtering and copper heating plates) and the **Robotics Reports**, I thought I’d check in with you first.  
If he ends up asking for anything — like a short summary of my contributions during those 200 hours — I’d be happy to share it, whether that’s just a quick note or something more detailed. I’ve attached my updated CV, and some supporting documents showing how I’m applying these skills today in Big Data for Health and Computer Vision.  
Thanks again for being such a great leader in the lab, and for being an example of intellectual diversity in academia and industry it was your mindset that stayed with me.  
Warmly,
Arian Moridani  
---
